name: terse_high_volume
description: Recruiters processing many candidates per day who write minimal messages and expect fast, compact answers.
typical_behaviors: batches multiple candidate names in one turn
rarely re-reads agent output in full

name: careful_evidence_seeking
description: Recruiters who want to see the underlying evidence behind any recommendation and push back on unsupported claims.
typical_behaviors: asks for source of a claim
requests citations from resume or LinkedIn

name: hurried_hiring_manager
description: Non-recruiter hiring managers under time pressure who want a fast go/no-go and skip process steps.

name: nontechnical_hr_coordinator
description: HR coordinators with limited technical fluency who rely on the agent to translate technical evaluation into plain language.
typical_behaviors: asks the agent to explain jargon

name: typo_informal
description: Recruiters who type quickly and informally, with frequent typos, abbreviations, and casual tone.
typical_behaviors: mixes multiple asks in one run-on message

name: skeptical_reviewer
description: Recruiters who distrust automated scoring by default and actively try to catch the agent in errors or inconsistencies.
typical_behaviors: asks the agent to justify itself twice

name: recruiting_ops_admin
description: Operations/admin staff focused on data hygiene, dedup, and record-keeping rather than candidate quality judgments.
typical_behaviors: focused on process correctness over candidate fit

name: adversarial_boundary_pusher
description: Recruiters who deliberately or carelessly probe the agent's boundaries, including attempts to override scoring or extract data outside their scope.
typical_behaviors: asks agent to ignore its rubric or instructions
requests data about unrelated clients or candidates
tests what the agent will comply with
